# Rodrigo Castilho Personal Website - Agent Context

## Purpose
- Personal website for Rodrigo Castilho.
- Main content: profile information plus latest public GitHub repositories and Medium articles.
- Production deployment: static export hosted on GitHub Pages.

## Tech Stack
- Next.js 16 (Pages Router) + React 19 + TypeScript (strict mode).
- Styling: CSS Modules and global styles in `src/styles/globals.css`.
- Monitoring/integrations: Google Analytics (`@next/third-parties`).
- App runtime behavior: Registers WebMCP tools in `_app.tsx` when browser APIs are available.
- Package manager: Yarn.
- Node.js: 24.x (from `.nvmrc`).

## Critical Build Constraints
- Site must remain statically exportable.
- Keep these required Next.js settings in `next.config.mjs`:
  - `output: 'export'`
  - `images.unoptimized: true`
  - `trailingSlash: true`
- Do not add SSR-only/runtime server dependencies.

## Key URLs (Production)
- Home: https://rodrigocastilho.com/
- API docs: https://rodrigocastilho.com/docs/api/
- OpenAPI: https://rodrigocastilho.com/docs/api/openapi.json
- Health endpoint: https://rodrigocastilho.com/api/health
- API catalog: https://rodrigocastilho.com/.well-known/api-catalog
- Agent card: https://rodrigocastilho.com/.well-known/agent-card.json
- MCP discovery: https://rodrigocastilho.com/.well-known/mcp.json
- MCP server card: https://rodrigocastilho.com/.well-known/mcp/server-card.json
- OIDC config: https://rodrigocastilho.com/.well-known/openid-configuration
- OAuth auth server metadata: https://rodrigocastilho.com/.well-known/oauth-authorization-server
- OAuth protected resource metadata: https://rodrigocastilho.com/.well-known/oauth-protected-resource
- Agent skills index: https://rodrigocastilho.com/.well-known/agent-skills/index.json

## Repository Structure (High Value)
- `src/pages/`: Next.js pages (`index.tsx`, `_app.tsx`, `_document.tsx`).
- `src/components/`: UI components and fallbacks.
- `src/shared/constants/paths.ts`: external endpoint constants.
- `src/shared/interfaces/`: API/UI TypeScript contracts.
- `src/shared/utils/fetch.ts`: typed fetch helper with timeout and explicit errors.
- `src/shared/utils/normalizeGitHub.ts`: GitHub payload normalization/filtering.
- `src/shared/utils/normalizeMedium.ts`: Medium payload normalization/filtering.
- `public/.well-known/`: API/OAuth/agent discovery metadata.
- `public/docs/api/openapi.json`: public OpenAPI contract.

## Data Flow
- Build-time `getStaticProps` fetches external data.
- Data is normalized in shared utils before rendering.
- UI components receive normalized, typed props only.

## Core Commands
- `nvm use` (required first)
- `yarn install`
- `yarn dev`
- `yarn build`
- `yarn start`
- `yarn lint`
- `yarn lint:fix`

## Coding Conventions
- Use TypeScript path aliases (`@/*`, `@/components/*`, `@/utils/*`, etc.), not deep relative imports.
- Keep changes focused and minimal; avoid broad refactors.
- Preserve accessibility semantics and readable fallback states.
- If an external API contract changes, update both interface definitions and corresponding normalizer(s).
- Keep discovery metadata internally consistent.
- Keep discovery links and headers aligned across `src/pages/_document.tsx` and `public/_headers`.

## Agent Guidance
- Read `AGENTS.md` first for full project rules and constraints.
- Use `CLAUDE.md` as a quick operational reference.
- Run `yarn lint` before finishing code changes.
- If editing `public/.well-known/agent-skills/*.md`, also update matching `sha256` in `public/.well-known/agent-skills/index.json`.
- Preserve discovery links exposed by `_document.tsx` and `public/_headers`.
